name: Ammunition
desc: When you attack with a ranged weapon that has the Ammunition property, you must have a piece of ammunition to use.
You draw the ammunition from a quiver, cartridge, or other container.
Each weapon that uses ammunition deals its normal damage on a hit.
If you attack with a ranged weapon and have no ammunition, the attack has no effect.

name: Finesse
desc: When making an attack with a melee weapon that has the Finesse property, you can use your Dexterity modifier instead of your Strength modifier.
You still use your Strength modifier for the damage roll.

name: Heavy
desc: Small creatures have Disadvantage on attack rolls with weapons that have the Heavy property.
Each time the attack roll with a Heavy weapon fails, the attacker has Disadvantage on the next attack roll with that weapon.

name: Light
desc: When you take the Attack action and attack with a light weapon, you can make one additional attack with a different light weapon as a Bonus Action.

name: Loading
desc: Due to the time required to load this weapon, you can fire only one piece of ammunition from it when you use an action, Bonus Action, or Reaction to fire it, regardless of how many actions you have.

name: Reach
desc: When you attack with a weapon that has the Reach property, your attack range is 10 feet greater than normal.

name: Thrown
desc: If a weapon has the Thrown property, you can throw the weapon to make a ranged attack.
If the weapon is a melee weapon, you use the same ability modifier for that attack roll and damage roll that you would use for a melee attack with the weapon.

name: Two-Handed
desc: This weapon can be used effectively with only two hands.

name: Versatile
desc: This weapon can be used with one or two hands.
A damage value in parentheses appears in the property.
That is the damage the weapon deals when it is used with two hands to make a melee attack.

name: Mastery
desc: Weapons with the Mastery property allow you to perform special maneuvers.
These maneuvers are detailed in the class features or feats that grant access to them.

name: Push
desc: When you hit a creature with a weapon that has the Push property, you can push the target up to 15 feet away from you.

name: Sweep
desc: When you hit a creature with a weapon that has the Sweep property, you can also damage another creature within 5 feet of the original target.

name: Topple
desc: When you hit a creature with a weapon that has the Topple property, you can knock the target prone.

name: Trip
desc: When you hit a creature with a weapon that has the Trip property, you can attempt to trip the target.
